operating_state: Total revenues increased 57% to $13.76 billion in Q3 2021, driven by 101,183 additional Model 3 and Model Y deliveries despite fewer Model S and Model X deliveries due to new version production ramp. Energy generation and storage revenues increased 39% quarterly from higher Megapack, solar, and Powerwall deployments. Regulatory credit sales decreased to $279 million.
cost_structure: Total automotive gross margin improved to 30% from 28% quarterly, driven by favorable Model 3 and Model Y production mix as Gigafactory Shanghai ramped with lower material, manufacturing, and freight costs from localized procurement. Energy generation and storage gross margin declined to 0% quarterly due to higher proportion of Solar Roof operating at lower margins during manufacturing ramp. Manufacturing costs face upward pressure from expedited delivery expenses for critical parts and increased labor costs due to pandemic-related shortages.
material_events: Invested $1.50 billion in bitcoin in Q1 2021 and accepted bitcoin payments before suspending the practice in May 2021, recording $101 million impairment losses and $128 million gains from sales during nine months. Redeemed Senior Notes due 2025 with $60 million extinguishment charge. Tesla Energy Operations entered amendment to update job and investment milestones under Research & Development Alliance agreement due to COVID-19 pandemic impacts.
macro_exposures: Global semiconductor and component shortages continue affecting production, requiring additional workaround manufacturing solutions that may be difficult to sustain. COVID-19 pandemic creates ongoing logistics and supply chain cost increases including port congestion and supplier delays. Foreign exchange fluctuations impact operations with significant foreign currency balances primarily in Chinese yuan, euros, and Canadian dollars.
forward_outlook: Capital expenditures expected to exceed $6 billion in 2021 and range $5-7 billion annually for next two fiscal years supporting global manufacturing expansion. Production growth dependent on Gigafactory Berlin and Texas construction completion and development of proprietary battery cell manufacturing with high-volume output and lower costs.
uncertainty_profile: Manufacturing ramp uncertainties at new international facilities may be exacerbated by concurrent global projects and component constraints requiring additional production workarounds. Digital asset holdings subject to volatile market prices with potential impairment charges despite no upward revisions until sale. Ongoing government investigations with potential for liability, penalties and sanctions though no agency has concluded wrongdoing occurred.
